what: Comparing Methods Using a Shared Cross-Validation Split with tune_imp()
title: slideimp
text: Simulate data Instead random NA sampling inside tune_imp(), internally calls sample_na_loc(), can generate NA locations front pass tune_imp(). na_loc 5 elements (5 repeats) row stores row column index missing value. can compare 1) PCA, 2) K-NN imputation, 3) custom method, since cross-validation missing values methods. Note: custom function requires first argument obj, must return object dimensions, subsequent arguments must match column names parameters data.frame. Pick method lowest cross-validation error:
code: # 20 rows, 1000 columns, all columns have at least some NA sim_obj <- sim_mat(n = 20, p = 1000, perc_col_na = 1) obj <- sim_obj$input obj[1:4, 1:4] #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.3722729 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 #> sample4 0.1734418        NA 0.0000000 0.0000000 na_loc <- sample_na_loc(obj, n_cols = 200, n_rows = 5, n_reps = 5) length(na_loc) #> [1] 5 na_loc[[1]][1:6, ] #>      row col #> [1,]  18 661 #> [2,]  17 661 #> [3,]  20 661 #> [4,]  15 661 #> [5,]   6 661 #> [6,]  10 293 # This custom function imputes missing values with random normal values and takes # `mean` and `sd` as params rnorm_imp <- function(obj, mean, sd) {   na <- is.na(obj)   obj[na] <- rnorm(sum(na), mean = mean, sd = sd) # <- impute values with rnorm   return(obj) # <- return an imputed object with the same dim as obj }  pca_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = "pca_imp",   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(ncp = 10) ) #> Tuning pca_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  knn_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = "knn_imp",   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(k = 10) ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  rnorm_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = rnorm_imp,   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(mean = 0, sd = 1) # must match with arguments of `rnorm_imp` ) #> Tuning custom function #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning mean(compute_metrics(pca_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 0.2048011 mean(compute_metrics(knn_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 0.1962497 mean(compute_metrics(rnorm_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 1.110258

what: Group-Wise Imputation with Small-Group Padding and Group-Wise Parameters with group_imp()
title: slideimp
text: group_imp() allows imputation performed separately within defined groups (e.g., chromosome), significantly reduces run time can increase accuracy K-NN PCA imputation. features: required - list-column element character vector variable names imputed together. aux: optional - auxiliary variables include group. used augment imputation quality features imputed . one group small (e.g., chrM), aux used pad group randomly drawing samples groups meet min_group_size. parameters: optional - group-specific imputation parameters. First simulate data 2 groups. create group3 1 feature show min_group_size pads using aux list column. generate group parameter group_imp() front. can see group3 padded 10 columns. group, also assign different number nearest neighbors demonstration. Finally, impute obj using modified group_imp_df. k = 10 passed group_imp() ignored since groups group-wise k specified.
code: sim_obj <- sim_mat(n = 20, p = 50, n_col_groups = 2)  # Matrix to be imputed obj <- sim_obj$input obj[1:5, 1:4] #>           feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4 #> sample1 0.03031619 0.1352710 0.4653579 0.1143298 #> sample2 0.50962966 0.2313022 0.9737881 0.5198844 #> sample3 0.69068459 0.5020337 0.7863144 0.9997625 #> sample4 0.40710170 0.4094603 0.3228294 0.2529592 #> sample5 0.73113251 0.4108405 0.6385945        NA  # Metadata, i.e., which features belong to which group meta <- sim_obj$col_group meta[1:5, ] #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group1 #> 2 feature2 group1 #> 3 feature3 group1 #> 4 feature4 group2 #> 5 feature5 group2  # We put feature 1 in `group3` meta[1, 2] <- "group3" meta[1:5, ] #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group3 #> 2 feature2 group1 #> 3 feature3 group1 #> 4 feature4 group2 #> 5 feature5 group2 set.seed(1234) group_imp_df <- prep_groups(colnames(obj), group = meta, min_group_size = 10) group_imp_df$parameters <- list(list(k = 3), list(k = 4), list(k = 5)) group_imp_df #> # slideimp table: 3 x 4 #>   group          feature             aux parameters #>  group1 <character [26]> <character [0]> <list [1]> #>  group2 <character [23]> <character [0]> <list [1]> #>  group3  <character [1]> <character [9]> <list [1]> knn_results <- group_imp(obj, group = group_imp_df, cores = 4, k = 10) #> Imputing 3 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)... print(knn_results, p = 4) #> Method: group_imp (KNN imputation) #> Dimensions: 20 x 50 #>  #>           feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4 #> sample1 0.03031619 0.1352710 0.4653579 0.1143298 #> sample2 0.50962966 0.2313022 0.9737881 0.5198844 #> sample3 0.69068459 0.5020337 0.7863144 0.9997625 #> sample4 0.40710170 0.4094603 0.3228294 0.2529592 #> sample5 0.73113251 0.4108405 0.6385945 0.4472338 #> sample6 0.48993078 0.4950764 0.6553896 0.6737998 #>  #> # Showing [1:6, 1:4] of full matrix

previous_headings: Sliding Window Imputation for WGBS/EM-seq Data with slide_imp()
what: Select window_size, overlap_size, and PCA/K-NN Parameters
title: slideimp
text: Note: WGBS/EM-seq data grouped chromosome performing sliding window imputation. , simulate 1000 sites. Importantly, simulate location vector feature (genomic position) varying spacing 50 500 bp apart . locations vector contains genomic position feature (column). used determine columns grouped together given window size. First, convert data beta matrix features columns. , real dataset, tune hyperparameters using chr22. , demonstration, use whole data since size small. ncp (number principal components) 2 4, indicating performing sliding PCA imputation. Pass k sliding K-NN imputation. window_size 5,000 10,000 bp. overlap_size fixed 1,000 bp (affect results much real analyses). Finally, can use slide_imp() impute full beta_matrix. Use best parameter combination cross-validation metrics. start end window location vectors. window_n number features included window. Turn dry_run impute data
code: set.seed(1234) sample_names <- paste0("S", 1:10) n_sites <- 1000  # Simulate positions with 50–500 bp between each site distances_between <- sample(50:500, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE) locations <- cumsum(distances_between) # <- important, location vector  methyl <- data.frame(   chr = "chr1",   start = locations,   end = locations,   strand = "+" )  for (i in seq_along(sample_names)) {   methyl[[paste0("numCs", i)]] <- sample.int(100, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE)   methyl[[paste0("numTs", i)]] <- sample.int(100, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE)   methyl[[paste0("coverage", i)]] <- methyl[[paste0("numCs", i)]] + methyl[[paste0("numTs", i)]] }  methyl[1:5, 1:10] #>    chr start  end strand numCs1 numTs1 coverage1 numCs2 numTs2 coverage2 #> 1 chr1   333  333      +     83     68       151     84     36       120 #> 2 chr1   718  718      +     82      2        84     54     99       153 #> 3 chr1  1173 1173      +     48      2        50     44     16        60 #> 4 chr1  1323 1323      +     70     48       118     48     56       104 #> 5 chr1  1483 1483      +     88     15       103     83     19       102 numCs_matrix <- as.matrix(methyl[, paste0("numCs", seq_along(sample_names))]) cov_matrix <- as.matrix(methyl[, paste0("coverage", seq_along(sample_names))]) beta_matrix <- numCs_matrix / cov_matrix  colnames(beta_matrix) <- sample_names rownames(beta_matrix) <- methyl$start  beta_matrix <- t(beta_matrix) # Set 10% of the data to missing set.seed(1234) beta_matrix[sample.int(length(beta_matrix), floor(length(beta_matrix) * 0.1))] <- NA beta_matrix[1:4, 1:4] #>           333       718       1173      1323 #> S1 0.54966887 0.9761905 0.96000000 0.5932203 #> S2 0.70000000 0.3529412 0.73333333 0.4615385 #> S3 0.06521739 0.7053571         NA 0.6507937 #> S4 0.69444444 0.2846154 0.04347826 0.7636364 params <- expand.grid(ncp = c(2, 4), window_size = c(5000, 10000)) params$overlap_size <- 1000 params$min_window_n <- 20 # windows with less than 20 columns are dropped  # Increase n_reps from 2 in actual analyses and use another chromosome (i.e., chr22) tune_slide_pca <- tune_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   parameters = params,   .f = "slide_imp",   n_reps = 2,   location = locations ) #> Tuning slide_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> ℹ Using default `num_na` = 500 (~5% of cells). #>   Increase for more reliability or decrease if missing is dense. #> Running Mode: sequential... #>  #> Step 2/2: Tuning  metrics <- compute_metrics(tune_slide_pca)  aggregate(.estimate ~ .metric + ncp + window_size, data = metrics, FUN = mean) #>   .metric ncp window_size .estimate #> 1     mae   2        5000 0.2257168 #> 2    rmse   2        5000 0.2838435 #> 3     mae   4        5000 0.2395280 #> 4    rmse   4        5000 0.2984013 #> 5     mae   2       10000 0.2123151 #> 6    rmse   2       10000 0.2642956 #> 7     mae   4       10000 0.2223801 #> 8    rmse   4       10000 0.2756162 slide_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   location = locations,   window_size = 5000,   overlap_size = 1000,   ncp = 2,   min_window_n = 20,   dry_run = TRUE # <- dry_run to inspect the windows ) #> Dropping 43 window(s) with fewer than 20 columns. #> # slideimp table: 24 x 4 #>  start end window_n  subset_local #>     15  34       20 <double [20]> #>    104 124       21 <double [21]> #>    188 209       22 <double [22]> #>    205 225       21 <double [21]> #>    222 241       20 <double [20]> #>    239 258       20 <double [20]> #>    254 273       20 <double [20]> #>    369 388       20 <double [20]> #>    385 404       20 <double [20]> #>    402 424       23 <double [23]> #> # ... with 14 more rows slide_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   location = locations,   window_size = 5000,   overlap_size = 1000,   ncp = 2,   min_window_n = 20,   dry_run = FALSE,   .progress = FALSE ) #> Method: slide_imp (PCA imputation) #> Dimensions: 10 x 1000 #> Note: requested columns still contain NA values #>  #>           333       718       1173      1323      1483       1925 #> S1 0.54966887 0.9761905 0.96000000 0.5932203        NA 0.08695652 #> S2 0.70000000 0.3529412 0.73333333 0.4615385 0.8137255 0.45454545 #> S3 0.06521739 0.7053571         NA 0.6507937 0.4393064 0.37735849 #> S4 0.69444444 0.2846154 0.04347826 0.7636364 0.9595960 0.54621849 #> S5 0.83505155 0.5777778 0.47517730        NA 0.7368421 0.21666667 #> S6 0.26612903 0.3451327 0.53072626 0.5000000 0.6465517 0.37288136 #>  #> # Showing [1:6, 1:6] of full matrix

text: Pass desired feature names subset argument. windows containing features imputed. Set flank = TRUE build windows centered feature subset. window extend window_size bp either side target feature (flanking mode). mode, overlap_size argument ignored. example, want impute features "1323" "33810" creating 5,000 bp flanking windows around feature:

title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: simulated beta matrix MSA microarray. CpGs columns. Chromosome-wise imputation Illumina microarrays can performed single function call (requires Illumina manifests, conveniently provided slideimp.extra package). example demonstrates PCA imputation. use K-NN imputation instead, supply k argument. Remove “ctl” probes (control probes) imputation obj <- obj[, !grepl("^ctl", colnames(obj))] set allow_unmapped = TRUE ignore . Deduplicate MSA EPICv2 data imputation slideimp.extra::dedup_matrix(). Speed K-NN imputation OpenMP using cores argument instead mirai (available default Windows Linux). need clock CpGs, provide subset argument ignore probes.
code: dim(MSA_beta_matrix) # [1]     20 281797 MSA_beta_matrix[1:4, 1:4] #         cg06185909_TC11 cg18975462_BC11 cg20516119_TC11 cg10149399_BC11 # sample1              NA       0.5023435       0.3835431              NA # sample2       0.4907466       0.5095459       0.9025816       0.4313347 # sample3       0.6885036              NA       0.7646753       0.4498772 # sample4       0.0000000       0.0000000              NA       0.0000000 library(slideimp.extra) library(slideimp) library(mirai)  imputed <- group_imp(   obj = MSA_beta_matrix,   group = "MSA", # <- this feature requires the `slideimp.extra` package   ncp = 10, # <- change to `k` for K-NN imputation   .progress = FALSE # <- turn on to monitor progress of longer running jobs ) # Found cleaned manifest for 'MSA' # Groups 24 dropped: no features remaining after matching obj columns. # Imputing 25 group(s) using PCA. # Running Mode: sequential ...  print(imputed, n = 4, p = 4) # Method: group_imp (PCA imputation) # Dimensions: 20 x 281797 # #         cg06185909_TC11 cg18975462_BC11 cg20516119_TC11 cg10149399_BC11 # sample1       0.1517542       0.5023435      0.38354308       0.2067731 # sample2       0.4907466       0.5095459      0.90258164       0.4313347 # sample3       0.6885036       0.7339375      0.76467530       0.4498772 # sample4       0.0000000       0.0000000      0.05230101       0.0000000 # # # Showing [1:4, 1:4] of full matrix

title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: simulate DNA methylation (DNAm) microarray data 2 chromosomes. slideimp functions expect input numeric matrix variables stored columns. Hyperparameters tuned using tune_imp(). evaluate following options grid search: Number nearest neighbors (k): 5 20 Inverse distance power (dist_pow) weighted averaging: 1 2 (higher values assign lower weights distant neighbors) Tuning performed subset data. use 10 repeats (n_reps = 10) cross-validation evaluation. artificially mask 50 observed values (num_na = 50) compute RMSE MAE. Use larger values n_reps num_na real analyses reliable error estimates. Note: Parallelization via cores argument available K-NN imputation OpenMP. Calculate errors using compute_metrics() {yardstick} functions. K-NN without OpenMP, PCA imputation, custom functions (see vignette), set parallelization mirai::daemons(). Note: machines multi-threaded BLAS, turn pin_blas = TRUE tuning PCA imputation parallel avoid thrashing. perform imputation group_imp() using best parameters. PCA imputation group_imp() can parallelized using mirai package, similar tune_imp(). pin_blas = TRUE can help PCA imputation multi-threaded BLAS machines. Alternatively, full matrix imputation can performed using knn_imp() pca_imp().
code: library(slideimp) set.seed(1234) sim_obj <- sim_mat(n = 20, p = 100, n_col_groups = 2)  # Extract the $input attribute obj <- sim_obj$input  # Extract metadata to map the features to groups group_df <- sim_obj$col_group knn_params <- expand.grid(k = c(5, 20), dist_pow = c(1, 2)) group2_columns <- subset(group_df, group == "group2") group2_only <- obj[, group2_columns$feature]  tune_knn <- tune_imp(   group2_only,   parameters = knn_params,   .f = "knn_imp",   cores = 8,   n_reps = 10,   num_na = 50 ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: parallel... #> Step 2/2: Tuning metrics <- compute_metrics(tune_knn)  # equivalently: dplyr::summarize(metrics, ..., .by = c(k, dist_pow, .metric)) sum_metrics <- do.call(   data.frame,   aggregate(     .estimate ~ k + dist_pow + .metric,     data = metrics,     FUN = function(x) {       c(         n = length(x),         mean_error = mean(x),         sd_error = sd(x)       )     }   ) )  sum_metrics[order(sum_metrics$.estimate.mean_error), ] #>    k dist_pow .metric .estimate.n .estimate.mean_error .estimate.sd_error #> 2 20        1     mae          10            0.1501427         0.01599598 #> 4 20        2     mae          10            0.1519464         0.01638402 #> 1  5        1     mae          10            0.1656087         0.01683512 #> 3  5        2     mae          10            0.1669792         0.01666404 #> 6 20        1    rmse          10            0.1897722         0.01915653 #> 8 20        2    rmse          10            0.1918765         0.01936392 #> 5  5        1    rmse          10            0.2081833         0.01949624 #> 7  5        2    rmse          10            0.2101955         0.01884442 mirai::daemons(2) # 2 Cores  # PCA imputation. pca_params <- data.frame(ncp = c(1, 5)) # For machines with multi-threaded BLAS, turn on `pin_blas = TRUE` tune_pca <- tune_imp(obj, parameters = pca_params, .f = "pca_imp", n_reps = 10, num_na = 50)  mirai::daemons(0) # Close daemons knn_group_results <- group_imp(obj, group = group_df, k = 20, dist_pow = 1, cores = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)... knn_group_results #> Method: group_imp (KNN imputation) #> Dimensions: 20 x 100 #>  #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 0.3924661 0.2434143 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.4788070 0.5061132 0.3923603 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 0.7634236 1.0000000 #> sample4 0.1734418 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.2106358 #> sample5 0.5388440 0.5306182 0.5685354 0.5383513 0.4680080 0.8518388 #> sample6 0.3768380 0.5570723 0.8764909 0.5276245 0.6722794 0.5740639 #>  #> # title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: slide_imp() performs sliding window imputation. function Illumina DNAm microarrays; see group_imp(). DNAm WGBS/EM-seq data grouped chromosomes (.e., run slide_imp() separately chromosome) imputation. See package vignette details. See vignette details selecting window_size overlap_size tune_imp(). slide_imp() parameters: location: required - sorted numeric vector length ncol(obj) specifying position column (e.g., genomic coordinates bp). window_size: required - width sliding window (unit location). overlap_size: optional - overlap width consecutive windows (units location). Must strictly less window_size. min_window_n: required - minimum number columns window must contain imputed. Windows fewer columns threshold dropped. Must greater k (K-NN) ncp (PCA). dry_run: optional - return just calculated windows examine windows included. k: required - (specifying K-NN imputation) number nearest neighbors use inside window. ncp: required - (specifying PCA imputation) number principal components retain. Use instead k performing sliding-window PCA imputation. subset: optional - impute just subset features (.e., just clock CpGs). flank: optional - build flanking windows window_size around features provided subset. First, let’s perform dry run examine windows imputed slide_imp. , can perform imputation using given parameters.

text: Choices "EPICv2" "MSA" (requires {slideimp.extra} package). (Basic): data.frame containing feature column (character strings feature names) group column (defining group). (Advanced): use prep_groups() create groups based mapping data.frame (.e., Illumina manifests) custom group-wise parameters. slide_imp(): Sliding window K-NN PCA imputation extremely high-dimensional numeric matrices (WGBS/EM-seq) ordered features (.e., genomic position). tune_imp(): Parallelizable hyperparameter tuning repeated cross-validation; works built-custom imputation functions. knn_imp(): Full-matrix K-NN imputation multi-core parallelization, {mlpack} KD/Ball-Tree nearest neighbor implementation (data low missing rates extremely high dimensions), optional subset imputation (ideal epigenetic clock calculations). pca_imp(): Optimized version missMDA::imputePCA() high-dimensional numeric matrices. col_vars() mean_imp_col() provide fast column-wise variance mean imputation using high-performance RcppArmadillo backend full OpenMP parallelization support.

title: Grouped K-NN or PCA Imputation — group_imp
text: obj numeric matrix samples rows features columns. group Specification features grouped imputation. Accepts three formats: character: string naming supported Illumina platform; see Note section. data.frame (Long format): group: Column identifying group feature. feature: Character column individual feature names. data.frame (List-column format): feature: List-column character vectors impute. row group. aux: (Optional) List-column auxiliary names used context. parameters: (Optional) List-column group-specific parameter lists. subset Character vector feature names impute (default NULL means impute features). Must subset obj_cn (colnames(obj)) must appear least one group's feature. Features group subset demoted auxiliary columns group. Groups left zero features demotion dropped message. allow_unmapped Logical. FALSE, every column colnames(obj) must appear group. TRUE, columns group assignment left untouched (neither imputed used auxiliary columns) message issued instead error. k Integer. Number nearest neighbors imputation. 10 good starting point. ncp Integer. Number components used predict missing entries. method K-NN imputation: distance metric use ("euclidean" "manhattan"). PCA imputation: regularization imputation algorithm ("regularized" "EM"). cores number OpenMP cores K-NN imputation . PCA mirai-based parallelism, use mirai::daemons() instead. .progress Show imputation progress (default TRUE). min_group_size Integer NULL. Minimum column count (features + aux) per group. Groups smaller padded randomly sampled columns obj. Passed prep_groups() internally. colmax Numeric. number 0 1. Threshold column-wise missing data rate imputation skipped. post_imp Boolean. Whether impute remaining missing values (failed imputation) using column means. dist_pow Numeric. amount penalization away nearest neighbors weighted average. dist_pow = 0 (default) simple average nearest neighbors. tree Logical. FALSE (default) uses brute-force K-NN. TRUE uses mlpack BallTree. max_cache Numeric. Maximum allowed cache size GB (default 4). greater 0, pairwise distances columns missing values pre-computed cached, faster moderate-sized data uses O(m^2) memory m number columns missing values. Set 0 disable caching trade speed lower memory usage. scale Logical. TRUE (default), variables scaled unit variance. coeff.ridge Numeric. Ridge regularization coefficient (default 1). used method = "regularized". Values < 1 regularize less (closer EM); values > 1 regularize (closer mean imputation). threshold Numeric. threshold assessing convergence. row.w Row weights (internally normalized sum 1). Can one : NULL (default): rows weighted equally. numeric vector: Custom positive weights length nrow(obj). "n_miss": Rows missing values receive lower weight. seed Numeric NULL. Random seed reproducibility. nb.init Integer. Number random initializations. first initialization always mean imputation. maxiter Integer. Maximum number iterations algorithm. miniter Integer. Minimum number iterations algorithm. pin_blas Logical. TRUE, pin BLAS threads 1 reduce contention using parallel PCA systems linked multi-threaded BLAS. na_check Boolean. Check leftover NA values results (internal use). on_infeasible Character, one "error" (default group_imp()), "skip", "mean" (default slide_imp()). Controls behaviour group infeasible imputation, e.g., k/ncp exceeds number usable columns applying colmax, subset columns group exceed colmax.

text: K-NN: use cores argument (requires OpenMP). mirai::daemons() active, cores automatically set 1 avoid nested parallelism. PCA: use mirai::daemons() instead cores. macOS, OpenMP typically unavailable cores falls back Use mirai::daemons() parallelization instead. Linux OpenBLAS MKL, set pin_blas = TRUE running parallel PCA prevent BLAS threads mirai workers competing cores.

title: Grouped K-NN or PCA Imputation — group_imp
code: # Generate example data with missing values set.seed(1234) to_test <- sim_mat(10, 20, perc_total_na = 0.05, perc_col_na = 1) obj <- to_test$input group <- to_test$col_group # metadata that maps `colnames(obj)` to groups head(group) #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group2 #> 2 feature2 group1 #> 3 feature3 group1 #> 4 feature4 group2 #> 5 feature5 group1 #> 6 feature6 group1  # Simple grouped K-NN imputation results <- group_imp(obj, group = group, k = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: sequential ...  # Impute only a subset of features subset_features <- sample(to_test$col_group$feature, size = 10) knn_subset <- group_imp(obj, group = group, subset = subset_features, k = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: sequential ...  # Use prep_groups() to inspect and tweak per-group parameters prepped <- prep_groups(colnames(obj), group) prepped$parameters <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(prepped)), \(i) list(k = 2)) prepped$parameters[[2]]$k <- 4 knn_grouped <- group_imp(obj, group = prepped, cores = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)... if (FALSE) { # interactive() && requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE) # PCA imputation with mirai parallelism mirai::daemons(2) pca_grouped <- group_imp(obj, group = group, ncp = 2) mirai::daemons(0) pca_grouped }

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
code: slide_imp(   obj,   location,   window_size,   overlap_size = 0,   flank = FALSE,   min_window_n,   subset = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   k = NULL,   cores = 1,   dist_pow = 0,   max_cache = 4,   ncp = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   coeff.ridge = 1,   seed = NULL,   row.w = NULL,   nb.init = 1,   maxiter = 1000,   miniter = 5,   method = NULL,   .progress = TRUE,   colmax = 0.9,   post_imp = TRUE,   na_check = TRUE,   on_infeasible = c("skip", "error", "mean") )

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: obj numeric matrix samples rows features columns. location sorted numeric vector length ncol(obj) giving position column (e.g., genomic coordinates). Used define sliding windows. window_size Window width units location. overlap_size Overlap consecutive windows units location. Must less window_size. Default 0. Ignored flank = TRUE. flank Logical. TRUE, instead sliding windows across whole matrix, one window width window_size created flanking feature listed subset. mode overlap_size ignored. Requires subset provided. Default = FALSE. min_window_n Minimum number columns window must contain imputed. Windows smaller imputed. k ncp must also smaller min_window_n. subset Character. Vector column names integer vector column indices specifying columns impute. dry_run Logical. TRUE, skip imputation return slideimp_tbl object windows used (dropping rules). k/ncp required mode. Columns: start, end, window_n, plus subset_local (list-column local subset indices) flank = FALSE, target subset_local flank = TRUE. Default = FALSE. k Integer. Number nearest neighbors imputation. 10 good starting point. cores Integer. Number cores K-NN parallelization (OpenMP). macOS, OpenMP may need additional compiler configuration. dist_pow Numeric. amount penalization away nearest neighbors weighted average. dist_pow = 0 (default) simple average nearest neighbors. max_cache Numeric. Maximum allowed cache size GB (default 4). greater 0, pairwise distances columns missing values pre-computed cached, faster moderate-sized data uses O(m^2) memory m number columns missing values. Set 0 disable caching trade speed lower memory usage. ncp Integer. Number components used predict missing entries. scale Logical. TRUE (default), variables scaled unit variance. coeff.ridge Numeric. Ridge regularization coefficient (default 1). used method = "regularized". Values < 1 regularize less (closer EM); values > 1 regularize (closer mean imputation). seed Integer. Random number generator seed. row.w Row weights (internally normalized sum 1). Can one : NULL (default): rows weighted equally. numeric vector: Custom positive weights length nrow(obj). "n_miss": Rows missing values receive lower weight. nb.init Integer. Number random initializations. first initialization always mean imputation. maxiter Integer. Maximum number iterations algorithm. miniter Integer. Minimum number iterations algorithm. method K-NN imputation: distance metric use ("euclidean" "manhattan"). PCA imputation: regularization imputation algorithm ("regularized" "EM"). .progress Show progress bar (default = TRUE). colmax Numeric. number 0 1. Threshold column-wise missing data rate imputation skipped. post_imp Boolean. Whether impute remaining missing values (failed imputation) using column means. na_check Boolean. Check leftover NA values results (internal use). on_infeasible Character, one "error" (default group_imp()), "skip", "mean" (default slide_imp()). Controls behaviour group infeasible imputation, e.g., k/ncp exceeds number usable columns applying colmax, subset columns group exceed colmax.

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: numeric matrix dimensions obj missing values imputed. dry_run = TRUE, returns data.frame class slideimp_tbl columns start, end, window_n, plus subset_local (target flank = TRUE).

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: sliding window approach divides input matrix smaller segments based location values applies imputation window independently. Values overlapping areas averaged across windows produce final imputed result. Two windowing modes supported: flank = FALSE (default): Greedily partitions location vector windows width window_size requested overlap_size consecutive windows. flank = TRUE: Creates one window per feature subset exactly flanks specific feature using supplied window_size. Specify k related arguments use knn_imp(), ncp related arguments pca_imp().

code: # Generate sample data with missing values with 20 samples and 100 columns # where the column order is sorted (i.e., by genomic position) set.seed(1234) beta_matrix <- sim_mat(20, 100)$input location <- 1:100  # It's very useful to first perform a dry run to examine the calculated windows windows_statistics <- slide_imp(   beta_matrix,   location = location,   window_size = 50,   overlap_size = 10,   min_window_n = 10,   dry_run = TRUE ) windows_statistics #> # slideimp table: 3 x 4 #>  start end window_n  subset_local #>      1  50       50 <double [50]> #>     41  90       50 <double [50]> #>     81 100       20 <double [20]>  # Sliding Window K-NN imputation by specifying `k` (sliding windows) imputed_knn <- slide_imp(   beta_matrix,   location = location,   k = 5,   window_size = 50,   overlap_size = 10,   min_window_n = 10,   scale = FALSE # This argument belongs to PCA imputation and will be ignored ) #> Step 1/2: Imputing #>  Processing window 1 of 3 #>  Processing window 2 of 3 #>  Processing window 3 of 3 #> Step 2/2: Averaging overlapping regions imputed_knn #> Method: slide_imp (KNN imputation) #> Dimensions: 20 x 100 #>  #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 0.3924661 0.2434143 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.4922511 0.5061132 0.3923603 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 0.7634236 1.0000000 #> sample4 0.1734418 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.2106358 #> sample5 0.4499620 0.5306182 0.5685354 0.5383513 0.4680080 0.8518388 #> sample6 0.3768380 0.5570723 0.8764909 0.5276245 0.6722794 0.5740639 #>  #> # title: Tune Parameters for Imputation Methods — tune_imp
code: # Setup example data. Increase `num_na` (500) and `n_reps` (10-30) in real # analyses obj <- sim_mat(10, 50)$input  # 1. Tune K-NN imputation with random NA injection params_knn <- data.frame(k = c(2, 4)) results <- tune_imp(obj, params_knn, .f = "knn_imp", n_reps = 1, num_na = 10) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning compute_metrics(results) #>   k param_set rep_id error  n n_miss .metric .estimator .estimate #> 1 2         1      1  <NA> 10      0     mae   standard 0.1744917 #> 2 2         1      1  <NA> 10      0    rmse   standard 0.2441705 #> 3 4         2      1  <NA> 10      0     mae   standard 0.1528578 #> 4 4         2      1  <NA> 10      0    rmse   standard 0.2000098  # 2. Tune with fixed NA positions na_positions <- list(   matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 1, 1, 1), ncol = 2),   matrix(c(2, 3, 4, 2, 2, 2), ncol = 2) )  results_fixed <- tune_imp(   obj,   data.frame(k = 2),   .f = "knn_imp",   na_loc = na_positions ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  # 3. Custom imputation function custom_fill <- function(obj, val = 0) {   obj[is.na(obj)] <- val   obj } tune_imp(obj, data.frame(val = c(0, 1)), .f = custom_fill, num_na = 10) #> Tuning custom function #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning #> # slideimp table: 2 x 5 #>  val param_set rep_id        result error #>    0         1      1 <df [10 x 2]>  <NA> #>    1         2      1 <df [10 x 2]>  <NA> if (FALSE) { # interactive() && requireNamespace("mirai", quietly = TRUE) # 4. Parallel tuning (requires mirai package) mirai::daemons(2) parameters_custom <- data.frame(mean = c(0, 1), sd = c(1, 1))  # Define a simple custom function for illustration custom_imp <- function(obj, mean, sd) {   na_pos <- is.na(obj)   obj[na_pos] <- stats::rnorm(sum(na_pos), mean = mean, sd = sd)   obj }  results_p <- tune_imp(   obj, parameters_custom,   .f = custom_imp, n_reps = 1, num_na = 10 ) mirai::daemons(0) # Close workers }

title: slideimp 1.0.0

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: group_imp() enforces stricter data validation. requested feature subset must subset object’s column names must subset mapping data.frame. Set allow_unmapped = TRUE bypass errors intersections incomplete. group_imp() tune_imp() now error arguments supplied apply chosen imputation method, rather silently ignoring . knn_imp() now uses logical tree argument toggle Ball tree (TRUE) brute force (FALSE). KD tree longer supported. knn_imp() pca_imp() gain early errors early exits. pca_imp() gains colmax post_imp arguments knn_imp(). prep_groups() (formerly group_features()) new name grouping function. now accepts column name vector instead full matrix. sample_na_loc() (formerly inject_na()) now exported. original remains accessible via slideimp:::inject_na() legacy code. sim_mat() now returns matrix sample--column format immediate compatibility package functions. perc_NA renamed perc_total_na, dimensions now specified via n (rows) p (columns). tune_imp() gains unified method argument applies pca_imp() knn_imp(), replacing pca_method knn_method. rep argument renamed n_reps. tune_imp() results v0.5.4 longer reproducible internal NA generation now uses sample_na_loc(). khanmiss1 dataset removed.

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: compute_metrics() now supports data frames result list column containing truth estimate columns, similar yardstick. group_imp() prep_groups() automatically look Illumina manifests using register--load pattern {slideimp.extra}. knn_imp() gains max_cache control internal cache size (defaults 4GB). sim_mat() gains rho argument support compound symmetry correlation structures simulated matrices. sim_mat() tune_imp() gain dedicated print methods provide concise summaries instead dumping raw data console. slide_imp() gains location, flank, dry_run arguments fixed-window imputation, “flank mode” features surrounding subset, pre-computation inspection window statistics. tune_imp() gains granular control NA injection via n_cols, n_rows, num_na, na_col_subset. Pre-calculated locations can also passed na_loc compare methods using identical NA patterns.

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: col_vars() mean_imp_col() overhauled use faster RcppArmadillo backend now support parallel computation OpenMP. Dependencies streamlined. tibble purrr removed hard dependencies, cli added informative messaging, carrier added explicit dependency. Documentation thoroughly overhauled numerous consistency improvements bug fixes. RhpcBLASctl added suggested package allow pinning BLAS cores avoid thrashing parallel runs. group_imp() tune_imp() prioritize process-level parallelization via mirai. knn_imp() supports OpenMP-controlled parallelization via cores argument mirai daemons active. knn_imp() pca_imp() use optimized internal Rcpp functions better performance.

title: slideimp 0.5.4

title: slideimp 0.5.3
text: Initial CRAN submission.
